$comment: TKT-269 / PLAN-011 shared cross-language parity corpus. Both the Python emitter (scripts/checks/parser_parity_emitter.py) and the vitest guard (packages/domain/src/domain/parser-parity.test.ts) read THIS file. Each vector pins BOTH languages' output; a legitimate current difference carries distinct columns + an allowedDivergence reason (see parser-domain-parity.md). Python's None is written in its normalised form ('standard' for case-type). Any behavioural change to one side must update the other column here in the same change.

vrmVectors.name: ocr-extra-digit
vrmVectors.allowedDivergence: D1 — Python normalize_vrm strips an intrusive OCR '1' in the LL1DDLLL shape; the TS canonicaliser has no extra-digit special-case.

vrmVectors.name: hyphenated
vrmVectors.allowedDivergence: D2 — Python strips only whitespace (\s), keeping the hyphen; TS strips all [^A-Z0-9].

vrmVectors.name: dotted
vrmVectors.allowedDivergence: D2 — Python keeps the dot separator; TS strips all non-alphanumerics.

markerVectors.name: marker-then-digit
markerVectors.allowedDivergence: D3 — Python's marker regex requires a trailing alphabetic char (guards 'A.4' numeric noise); the TS parser matches the marker prefix regardless of the next char.

markerVectors.name: total-loss-then-digits
markerVectors.allowedDivergence: D3 — same trailing-alpha guard: Python rejects a marker followed by digits; TS accepts the prefix.

markerVectors.name: bare-marker
markerVectors.allowedDivergence: D4 — a bare marker with no body: Python returns no marker; TS's prefix match yields the case type.
